title: phragmites australis subsp. berlandieri
intro: Phragmites australis subsp. berlandieri, also known as Texas cordgrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help to reduce swelling and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Phragmites australis subsp. berlandieri include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. It can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
